D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Unsharp Mask — Sharpen by Adding High-Pass

out = I + α(I − G_σ ∗ I) — original plus α times the high-pass detail; named for 19th-century darkroom technique that used a blurred mask layered over the negative

Despite the name "unsharp", it sharpens. The "unsharp mask" is the blurred copy you subtract. α < 1 is subtle, 1 ≈ standard, 2 produces visible halos. σ chooses what scale of detail to boost — small σ for fine textures, large σ for tonal contrast.

Two sliders: σ for blur scale, α for amount.
